label: About
title: About Porcelain Chime Basin
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 55 recent saved-signal files and 77 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around Symphony of Sounds Are Unleashed as Porcelain Bowls Float and Clang in Giant Basin Installation and Clinamen Installation by Céleste Boursier-Mougenot, plus 7 more. A source image from mymodernmet.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Porcelain Chime Basin: Make one finished still image as a textile field seen through an overhead scan: a broad pale basin-water surface, almost empty, with irregular porcelain-bowl marks drifting like quiet atoms. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against left red translucent membrane slit, right red translucent membrane pocket, and porcelain ripple rings in basin skin.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
